# Assistant Director - Applied AI Engineer
[...]
Line of business Applied AI
[...]
At Moody's, we unite the brightest minds to turn today’s risks into tomorrow’s opportunities. We do this by striving to create an inclusive environment where everyone feels welcome to be who they are—with the freedom to exchange ideas, think innovatively, and listen to each other and customers in meaningful ways. Moody’s is transforming how the world sees risk. As a global leader in ratings and integrated risk assessment, we’re advancing AI to move from insight to action—enabling intelligence that not only understands complexity but responds to it. We decode risk to unlock opportunity, helping our clients navigate uncertainty with clarity, speed, and confidence.
[...]
Skills and Competencies
[...]
- Strong generalist engineering experience with the ability to deliver reliable, scalable software across the full technology stack
[...]
- Demonstrated ability to own technical decisions, evaluate tradeoffs, and lead technical design when required
[...]
- Experience building and shipping full-stack applications using modern frameworks and tooling
[...]
- Practical experience applying modern AI and machine learning techniques in production environments
[...]
- Ability to translate
[...]
into well-designed
[...]
- Deep expertise in artificial intelligence, with a track record of implementing advanced AI solutions—including agentic AI systems—to drive strategic transformation and operational efficiency
[...]
This role focuses on identifying high-impact business problems and designing, building, and owning applied AI solutions that improve real workflows for internal and external users.
[...]
- Identify high-friction problems across the business and design software solutions that address them effectively
[...]
- Build end-to-end, full-stack applications powered by modern AI tooling
[...]
- Own technical design and direction for assigned projects and clearly communicate architectural decisions and tradeoffs
[...]
- Partner closely with users to understand workflows and translate insights into product and engineering decisions
[...]
- Deploy solutions to production environments and take responsibility for ongoing performance, reliability, and improvements
[...]
- Review, test, and approve code to ensure quality, consistency, and maintainability
[...]
- Mentor and support other engineers, contributing to the team’s technical growth
[...]
The Applied AI team at Moody’s focuses on solving real, high-impact problems by embedding AI into everyday workflows. The team works closely with business partners across the organization to design and deliver practical, production-ready solutions that help people access the right information at the right time and make better decisions.

highlights:
Machine Learning Research Scientist; Remote | Quickswoop
[...]
# Machine Learning Research Scientist; Remote

Remote, USA

Posted 2026-06-05

Full-time
[...]
Position: Machine Learning Research Scientist (Remote) Location: New York In the Predictive Analytics AI group, we build data-driven, highly distributed machine learning systems. Our engineers and researchers are responsible for architecting and developing these ML services end-to-end overcoming unique challenges that involve building systems that have high throughput availability, consistency, and low latency. The Predictive Analytics AI Group is the central group in Moody’s Analytics comprising of researchers and engineers working together to build data-driven customer-facing products, as well as the necessary infrastructure to support the ML services following the industry leading practices. The group has worked on and built some award-winning AI products like Compliance Catalyst, Adverse Media Monitoring, Corona pulse, Quiqspread, News Edge 2.0, ESG and has participated in various internal automation initiatives. The group also regularly publish and present their work in top-tier academic and industry conferences. We have a flexible work environment and allow remote work depending on one’s personal choice. Broadly, we are looking for colleagues who are passionate about: • Natural language processing • Information retrieval • Information extraction • Graph Neural Networks • Recommender systems • Knowledge graphs • Explainable AI We'll trust you to: • Collaborate with colleagues on production systems and applications • Design, experiment, and evaluate algorithms as well as models using PyTorch, scikit-learn, Tensorflow, Hugging Face • Work on POCs and research prototypes • Provide thought leadership in machine learning • Represent Moody’s Analytics at scientific and industry conferences • Lead collaboration with colleagues and academia to publish research findings in leading academic venues such as ACL, EMNLP, NAACL-HLT, AAAI, KDD, CIKM, SIGIR, ECIR You’ll need to have: • Ph.D. in CS, ML, Math, Statistics, Engineering, Quant, or relevant industry experience. • Publication record in top-tier academic conferences and journals • Proficiency in modern programming languages such as Python • Proficiency in leading research projects Nice to have: • Experience with MLOPs technologies and workflows • Experience in working with engineering teams on taking research prototypes to production #J-18808-Ljbffr

# Machine Learning Research Scientist; Remote

Posted May 27, 2026

Position: Machine Learning Research Scientist (Remote) Location: New York In the Predictive Analytics AI group, we build data-driven, highly distributed machine learning systems. Our engineers and researchers are responsible for architecting and developing these ML services end-to-end overcoming unique challenges that involve building systems that have high throughput availability, consistency, and low latency. The Predictive Analytics AI Group is the central group in Moody’s Analytics comprising of researchers and engineers working together to build data-driven customer-facing products, as well as the necessary infrastructure to support the ML services following the industry leading practices. The group has worked on and built some award-winning AI products like Compliance Catalyst, Adverse Media Monitoring, Corona pulse, Quiqspread, News Edge 2.0, ESG and has participated in various internal automation initiatives. The group also regularly publish and present their work in top-tier academic and industry conferences. We have a flexible work environment and allow remote work depending on one’s personal choice. Broadly, we are looking for colleagues who are passionate about: • Natural language processing • Information retrieval • Information extraction • Graph Neural Networks • Recommender systems • Knowledge graphs • Explainable AI We'll trust you to: • Collaborate with colleagues on production systems and applications • Design, experiment, and evaluate algorithms as well as models using PyTorch, scikit-learn, Tensorflow, Hugging Face • Work on POCs and research prototypes • Provide thought leadership in machine learning • Represent Moody’s Analytics at scientific and industry conferences • Lead collaboration with colleagues and academia to publish research findings in leading academic venues such as ACL, EMNLP, NAACL-HLT, AAAI, KDD, CIKM, SIGIR, ECIR You’ll need to have: • Ph.D. in CS, ML, Math, Statistics, Engineering, Quant, or relevant industry experience. • Publication record in top-tier academic conferences and journals • Proficiency in modern programming languages such as Python • Proficiency in leading research projects Nice to have: • Experience with MLOPs technologies and workflows • Experience in working with engineering teams on taking research prototypes to production #J-18808-Ljbffr

results.title: Lead AI/ML Model Developper | Jobflarely
highlights:
Lead AI/ML Model Developper | Jobflarely

Jobflarely.← All Jobs

# Lead AI/ML Model Developper

Posted May 28, 2026

### About the position

Firm Risk Management Morgan Stanley's Firm Risk Management (FRM) Division is an exciting and rapidly growing space. We support Morgan Stanley to achieve its business goals by partnering with business units across the Firm to realize efficient risk-adjusted returns, acting as a strategic advisor to the Board and protecting the Firm from exposure to losses as a result of credit, market, liquidity, operational, model and other risks. Background on the Position The role will reside within the Firm Risk Management's Risk Analytics area. Risk Analytics develops market risk analytics, credit risk analytics and scenario analytics models providing quantitative analysis on the Firm's risk exposures. By developing mathematical and statistical models, Risk Analytics calculates the risks associated with specified sets of financial positions and day-to-day operations. Morgan Stanley is seeking a Senior AI/ML Model Developer (Vice President) in the Risk AI Application team, which is responsible for implementing Firm Risk Management's AI-strategy.

- Responsibilities
- Manage and mentor a small development team, providing technical direction and day-to-day support
- Lead development of Python-based AI applications, including NLP pipelines, LLM tools, and other machine learning models, used in Risk Management workflows
- Apply software engineering techniques to build efficient, reliable, and maintainable solutions
- Contribute to model and system design decisions, ensuring the solutions are technically sound and appropriate for Risk Management use cases
- Write clean, well-structured, and well-tested Python code
- Follow standard development practices such as GitHub-based version control, code reviews, unit testing, and continuous integration
- Deploy solutions to production environments, not just development or prototype settings
- Own and manage relationships with Risk Management stakeholders to understand requirements and design AI solutions that meet their needs

- Requirements
- 5+ years of experience in AI/ML, software engineering, or related fields
- Graduate degree in Computer Science, Mathematics, Engineering, Statistics, Physics, or a related quantitative discipline
- Strong Python programming skills, including familiarity with Git-based versioning and collaborative coding workflows
- Experience developing and deploying AI/ML or analytical solutions in production environments
- Understanding of financial concepts and products, or willingness to learn them
- Strong communication skills and the ability to work with technical and non-technical stakeholders

- Nice-to-haves
- Experience managing or mentoring junior developers
- Familiarity with financial models or risk analytics frameworks

Apply for This Job

results.title: Data Scientist Associate- NLP & LLM – NLP People
highlights:
Data Scientist Associate- NLP & LLM – NLP People

## Overview

JPMAM Data Science is dedicated to enhancing and streamlining every stage of the investment process, from financial analysis and portfolio management to client services and advisory. We analyse extensive collections of data including financial documents, analyst reports, news articles, meeting notes, and client communications to empower data-driven decision making and process automation. We are seeking passionate data scientists who can apply the latest methodologies to generate real-time and actionable insights.

The candidate must excel in working in a highly collaborative environment together with the business stakeholders, technologists, and control partners to deploy solutions into production. A strong passion for machine learning is essential, along with a commitment to continuous learning, research, and experimentation with new innovations in the field. Candidates should possess a solid understanding of modern NLP and/or financial knowledge, hands-on implementation experience, strong analytical thinking, and a keen interest in applying advanced analytics to solve complex problems in finance and asset management.

Job Responsibilities

Design and implement advanced techniques such as semantic search, retrieval-augmented generation (RAG), named entity recognition (NER), prompt engineering, and personalization for content extraction, search, question answering, reasoning, and recommendation. Develop LLM, NLP, and ML solutions that address client requirements and drive business transformation. Work closely with partner teams—including Business, Technology, Product Management, Strategy, and Business Management—to deploy and scale developed models in production environments. Build comprehensive testing setups to evaluate model performance and ensure efficacy and reliability. Communicate results effectively to business stakeholders through written reports, visualizations, and presentations. Stay current with the latest research in LLM, ML, and data science, and proactively identify and leverage emerging techniques to drive ongoing enhancement.

Required Qualifications, Capabilities, and Skills

Advanced degree in Data Science, Computer Science, or Machine LearningProven experience applying NLP, LLM, and ML techniques to solve high-impact business problems. Proficiency in programming languages such as Python and familiarity with machine learning libraries and frameworks. Excellent communication skills and ability to work collaboratively in a fast-paced, dynamic environment. Strong analytical skills with an understanding of financial markets and asset management.

Preferred Qualifications, Capabilities and Skills.

results.title: Moodys Corporation AI Adoption Tracker | Larridin | AI Initiative Tracker
highlights:
Moody's Corporation has emerged as a leading example of large-scale AI transformation in financial services, positioning itself as an early mover in generative AI (GenAI) adoption [1][2]. The company has undergone a strategic pivot from traditional ratings agency to AI-powered integrated risk assessment firm [3], with Moody's Analytics now representing the larger segment generating $4.41 billion in revenue in 2024 [1]. The company has developed comprehensive AI solutions including the market-leading Moody's Research Assistant [4][5], established strategic partnerships with Microsoft and Google Cloud [6][7], and created 35 AI-driven agents to automate various tasks [3]. With over 53% of all job postings mentioning AI keywords, Moody's demonstrates exceptional organizational commitment to AI across both technical and non-technical roles, signaling a comprehensive transformation of its workforce and operations [21].
[...]

Remarkably, non-technical roles show nearly the same AI expectations (49.4%) as technical roles (66.7%), indicating comprehensive organizational AI transformation. Marketing leads at 81.8%, suggesting AI is becoming integral to customer-facing functions.

Generative artificial intelligence is transforming how we build products, serve clients, and operate at scale. In the Chief Data and Analytics Office, you will help turn advanced models into dependable, secure, and high-performing production services. You will work with partners across machine learning, cloud engineering, and site reliability engineering to deliver solutions with clear return on investment. If you enjoy hands-on engineering, real-world constraints, and high-impact delivery, this role is for you. As a Senior Associate, Generative AI Engineer in the Chief Data and Analytics Office, you will help design, build, and support production generative artificial intelligence products and reusable backend application programming interfaces used across the firm. You will combine large enterprise datasets with large language and multimodal models to deliver scalable, measurable solutions. You will collaborate closely with machine learning, cloud engineering, and site reliability engineering partners to ensure reliability, performance, and strong operational controls. results.title: VP, Artificial Intelligence - Products & Services | CareerSync
highlights:
# VP, Artificial Intelligence - Products & Services
[...]
About the Role & TeamIDC is seeking a transformative AI leader to define and execute our enterprise-wide AI strategy—embedding cutting-edge artificial intelligence across both internal operations and customer-facing products. This is a rare opportunity to shape how a global data and intelligence company harnesses AI, from agentic workflows and large language models to predictive analytics and AI-native product experiences. Reporting directly to the CTO, the VP of AI will lead a high-performing technical team, partner across business units, and drive measurable business impact through intelligent, responsible, and scalable AI solutions. The ideal candidate combines deep technical expertise in modern AI—including generative AI, LLMs, and agentic systems—with the strategic acumen to align AI investments to revenue growth and competitive differentiation. IDC sits at the intersection of proprietary data, expert human insight, and global technology reach—an ideal foundation for AI transformation. Key opportunity areas include: AI-augmented research & analyst productivity — deploying LLM-powered tools to accelerate data synthesis, report generation, and market forecasting. Intelligent data products — embedding predictive models and AI-driven insights directly into IDC's subscription and advisory offerings. Agentic content & marketing workflows — leveraging generative AI to personalize content, automate content pipelines, and optimize Foundry's MarTech capabilities. Enterprise AI infrastructure — building shared platforms, APIs, and MLOps practices that enable AI adoption at scale across all IDC business units. What You’ll Do
[...]
AI Strategy & Vision: Partner with the CEO, CTO, CIO, and business unit heads to define IDC's multi-year AI roadmap—spanning generative AI, agentic systems, predictive analytics, and AI-native product development. Technical Leadership: Architect and oversee IDC's AI platform and infrastructure, ensuring scalability, security, and alignment with enterprise data strategy. Product Delivery: Develop and execute IDC’s approach to agentic AI technology, thereby delivering the centerpiece of IDC Quanta, our cross-channel technology intelligence layer. Team Building: Recruit, develop, and retain a world-class AI team spanning ML engineers, data scientists, AI product managers, and MLOps specialists. Stakeholder Alignment: Translate complex AI capabilities into clear business value for executives and department heads; maintain transparent goals and resource planning. Ecosystem & Partnerships: Evaluate and manage AI technology partnerships, vendor relationships, and open-source contributions to accelerate IDC's AI capabilities. What You Bring
[...]
PhD. or Master's degree in Computer Science, AI/ML, Statistics, or a related quantitative field (or equivalent experience).
[...]
7+ years of experience in applied machine learning, AI engineering, or data science—with at least 3 years in senior technical leadership roles.
[...]
and platforms that generated measurable
[...]
Deep expertise in modern AI/ML and agentic/generative systems design.
[...]
Hands-on fluency with generative AI technologies: LLM fine-tuning, retrieval-augmented generation (RAG), prompt engineering, and agentic frameworks (e.g., LangChain, LlamaIndex, AutoGen).
[...]
Strong engineering foundation: Python, SQL, cloud ML platforms (AWS SageMaker, GCP Vertex AI, or Azure ML), and modern MLOps practices (CI/CD, model monitoring, feature stores).
[...]
in media, data/intelligence, B2B SaaS, or MarTech industries.

Familiarity with knowledge graphs, semantic search, or AI applications in content and market research workflows.
[...]
Experience with responsible AI frameworks, model governance, and AI regulatory compliance (EU AI Act, NIST AI RMF).
[...]

# Director of Data Governance - Data Modeling
[...]
At Moody's, we unite the brightest minds to turn today’s risks into tomorrow’s opportunities. We do this by striving to create an inclusive environment where everyone feels welcome to be who they are—with the freedom to exchange ideas, think innovatively, and listen to each other and customers in meaningful ways. Moody’s is transforming how the world sees risk. As a global leader in ratings and integrated risk assessment, we’re advancing AI to move from insight to action—enabling intelligence that not only understands complexity but responds to it. We decode risk to unlock opportunity, helping our clients navigate uncertainty with clarity, speed, and confidence.
[...]
- Prior experience analyzing and/or designing relational and non-relational data sets, especially
[...]
, commercial real estate
[...]
public registration and regulatory
[...]
, and the
[...]
between entities in such datasets.
[...]
- Experience and proficiency using SQL for data analysis, or similar AI enabled platforms.
[...]
Undergraduate/first-level degree (e.g., Bachelor’s) or graduate/second-level degree (e.g. MBA, Master’s) with an emphasis in finance, economics, computer science, data science, or related fields.
[...]
The Director of Data Governance – Data Modeling will play a pivotal role within Moody’s Analytics Data Estate Data Governance team. This position is responsible for designing, coordinating the review process, and maintaining comprehensive documentation for data models at all levels—conceptual, logical, and physical. The Director will collaborate closely with technology and application development teams to ensure data models are well-documented, accurate according to the policies and standards set by Data Stewards, and consistently updated to support evolving business and customer requirements.
[...]
- Define and manage the relationship model by establishing clear types, cardinality, directionality, and temporal behavior, ensuring relationships accurately represent real-world constraints rather than system limitations. Prevent inconsistencies across domains and maintain thorough justifications so relationships can be clearly explained to customers, withstand audits, and be reliably utilized by AI systems.
[...]
- Ensure the Data Estate Logical Data Model is AI-ready and interoperable by supporting stable identifiers, explicit relationships, and rich, explainable semantics. Define and implement modeling standards necessary for advanced AI applications such as Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG), Generative AI, and agentic workflows, proactively identifying and addressing model gaps that could lead to hallucinations, misinterpretations, or incorrect reasoning, in collaboration with governance and platform teams.
[...]
The Moody’s Data Governance team operates as a central capability within the Data Estate, focused on ensuring data is fit for purpose, trusted, and aligned to customer use cases across Moody’s Analytics. The team defines and enforces data governance standards by identifying critical data elements, designing logical data models, documenting lineage, and establishing field-level data quality policies that reflect how customers actually use the data. Organized around domain-based data steward pods and supported by shared functions such as QA design, metadata management, entitlements, and customer engagement, the team works closely with product, engineering, and data operations to clarify accountability, reduce overlap, and improve decision-making. Through this operating model, Data Governance drives higher data quality, transparency, and interoperability, while enabling scalable analytics, regulatory compliance, and AI-ready data across the enterprise.

By combining advanced machine learning and artificial intelligence (ML/AI) technologies, a global workforce of subject matter experts, and a high-security infrastructure, we’re helping usher in the promise of clean and optimized digital data to all industries. Innodata offers a powerful combination of both digital data solutions and easy-to-use, high-quality platforms.

Innodata is building a team of Language Data Scientists and Gen AI experts to help our customers advance GenAI applications. You will work hands-on with multi-modal and multi-lingual datasets and collaborate with cross-functional partners. You will use your experience with human and synthetic data workflows to drive innovation and continuous improvement. The ideal candidate must have the right mix of skills in (computational) linguistics and human evaluation tasks, data science, and data engineering.

You are an expert at working cross functionally with both technical and non-technical stakeholders. Despite ambiguity, you use your technical knowledge and experience of working with multiple stake holder to drive solutions. You bring a research-oriented mindset towards developing long-term excellence. # Moody's Publishes Generative AI Slide Deck on Agentic Workflows
[...]
Moody's Publishes Generative AI Slide Deck on Agentic Workflows
[...]
Seeking Alpha reports that on 2026-06-08 Moody's Corporation published a slide deck in conjunction with an event that discusses **generative AI strategy** and **agentic workflow solutions** aimed at enhancing customer insights. The Seeking Alpha page linked the deck and displays **Moody's Corporation** last price **443.96**, a **-1.64%** change at the time of the article. The slide deck content is presented as an event supplement; the article does not include verbatim executive quotes or detailed technical specifications for models or APIs.
[...]
Seeking Alpha reports that on 2026-06-08 **Moody's Corporation** published a slide deck in conjunction with an event that covers **generative AI strategy** and **agentic workflow solutions** intended to enhance customer insights. The Seeking Alpha posting includes a market snapshot showing **Moody's Corporation** last price **443.96**, change **-1.64%**. The Seeking Alpha article reproduces the slide deck notice but does not include direct quotes from Moody's executives or model-level technical details.
[...]
Seeking Alpha's item does not provide model names, API endpoints, dataset descriptions, or performance metrics. The slide deck is described at a high level in the article as focusing on generative AI and agentic workflows; specific engineering approaches, toolchains, or governance controls are not documented in the Seeking Alpha piece.
[...]
Editorial analysis: Companies in financial information and credit analytics increasingly present generative AI and agentic workflows as ways to accelerate insight synthesis and analyst productivity. For practitioners, these patterns commonly raise integration questions around data lineage, model explainability, and regulatory-compliant audit trails when AI outputs influence credit opinions or customer advice.
[...]
Reporting milestones that would provide actionable signals include publication of a technical whitepaper, named model or vendor partnerships, pilot case studies with performance figures, descriptions of data governance and compliance controls, or regulatory disclosures tied to AI-assisted workflows. Observers will also look for product updates in Moody's public filings or customer communications that carry technical appendices or implementation timelines.
[...]
A major credit-data vendor publishing a generative AI slide deck is notable for practitioners who integrate financial signals, but the Seeking Alpha article provides only high-level coverage without technical or performance details.

results.title: Optum Hiring Data Scientist | Jobformore
highlights:
Optum Hiring Data Scientist | Jobformore
[...]
# Data Scientist
[...]
Optum is seeking a Data Science professional who is passionate about applying machine learning, Generative AI, and Large Language Models (LLMs) to solve complex healthcare problems on a scale. Imagine working with massive volumes of healthcare data spanning clinical, claims, behavioral, and digital interactions – powering intelligent personalization, discovery, and decision support for millions of members, providers, and care teams.
[...]
At Optum, the Data Science mission is to transform healthcare data into robust, scalable AI solutions, including LLM based and Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG) systems that combine enterprise knowledge with advanced language models to deliver accurate, contextual, and trustworthy insights. These solutions help uncover deep usage patterns, member journeys, and care signals that inform and continuously evolve Optum’s digital products, clinical platforms, and healthcare services.
[...]
Your work will directly influence how healthcare consumers engage with digital experiences, how clinicians access timely insights, and how business and care strategies are shaped through data. By joining Optum’s data science team, you will be part of an exciting journey to build advanced ML and GenAI driven products, grounded in responsible AI principles, that operate at enterprise scale and create meaningful impact across the healthcare ecosystem.
[...]
- Design, develop, and deploy advanced analytics, machine learning, and Generative AI solutions to address complex healthcare and business problems using largescale structured and unstructured data
[...]
- Applies advanced techniques including statistics
[...]
- Build and optimize LLM based applications, including Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG) frameworks, to enable accurate, context aware, and explainable insights from enterprise healthcare data sources such as clinical notes, claims, provider data, and policy documents
[...]
- Develop, evaluate, and finetune machine learning and GenAI models, including prompt engineering, retrieval strategies, and model evaluation techniques to improve accuracy, relevance, and performance
[...]

[...]
- Support prototyping, validation, and deployment of GenAI and ML solutions, working with engineering teams to ensure solutions are scalable, secure, and maintainable. Continuously stay current with advancements in LLMs, GenAI frameworks, and healthcare AI trends, contributing innovative ideas and improvements to Optum’s capabilities

- Experience using Machine Learning Algorithms: Linear & Logistic Regression, Time Series analysis, Support Vector Machine, K-Means clustering, K-Nearest Neighbors Decision Trees, Random Forest, Naive Bayes, PCA, SVD, Artificial Neural Networks, Association Rules, Genetic Algorithm, Optimization, Bagging and Boosting algorithms etc.

- Understanding of data, schema, data model, machine learning and how to bring efficiency in big data related life cycle
[...]
- Proven skills to create opportunities using machine learning or artificial intelligence
[...]
Optum, part of UnitedHealth Group, is a global health services and technology company revolutionizing healthcare delivery through data analytics, pharmacy benefits, and digital health solutions that connect millions to better care. Headquartered in Eden Prairie, Minnesota, Optum serves patients, providers, payers, and pharmacies worldwide with innovative platforms for care optimization, claims processing, and population health management.

highlights:
AI Engineer | Wfh

# AI Engineer

Posted Jun 5, 2026

Lazard is one of the world’s preeminent financial advisory and asset management firms. The AI Engineer will work within the AI & Data Group to design, develop, and deploy advanced AI-driven applications and solutions to support the Firm’s financial advisory and asset management businesses.

### Responsibilities

- Build large-scale AI and machine learning projects that deliver actionable insights and drive innovation
- Collaborate closely with investment bankers, asset managers, data scientists, software engineers, and senior management across the Firm to deliver impactful solutions

Skills

- Bachelor's or advanced degree in Computer Science, AI, Data Science, Machine Learning, or related fields
- 1-3 years of experience developing large scale GenAI applications
- Expertise in Python and frameworks such as TensorFlow, PyTorch, and scikit-learn
- Deep knowledge of machine learning algorithms and experience in productionizing AI models
- Proficiency with frameworks for large language models (LLMs) such as LangChain, LlamaIndex, and VectorDBs
- Experience developing and deploying AI solutions using cloud platforms like Azure, AWS, or GCP
- Advanced knowledge of unstructured data processing, including NLP (e.g., sentiment analysis, entity recognition, topic modeling, and retrieval-augmented generation)
- Proficiency in APIs and microservices frameworks like FastAPI or Flask
- Demonstrated ability to query and analyze data from relational and NoSQL databases (e.g., PostgreSQL, MongoDB, ElasticSearch)
- Strong communication skills with the ability to translate technical findings into actionable business strategies for bankers, asset managers, and other stakeholders
- Passion for building innovative, high-quality AI-driven products
- Ability to work both independently and collaboratively
- Prior experience in the financial industry or a strong interest in finance
- Familiar with Databricks, Azure AI and Microsoft Copilot ecosystem
- Expertise in building advanced retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) systems and developing autonomous LLM agent workflows
- Familiarity with MLOps workflows and tools like MLflow, Kubeflow, or Vertex AI
- Experience with real-time AI applications and stream processing frameworks
- Demonstrated success in delivering AI solutions with measurable business impact
- Strong knowledge of advanced GenAI and communicating insights to non-technical audiences

### Benefits

- Comprehensive, competitive benefits

Company Overview

- Lazard is a financial advisory and asset management firm focused on income strategies, alternative investments, and private equity funds. It was founded in 1848, and is headquartered in New York, New York, USA, with a workforce of 1001-5000 employees. Its website is Company H1B Sponsorship

- Lazard has a track record of offering H1B sponsorships, with 37 in 2025, 27 in 2024, 24 in 2023, 34 in 2022, 24 in 2021, 36 in 2020. Please note that this does not guarantee sponsorship for this specific role.

# Senior/Lead - Backend Engineer, Data Engineer - AI Engineering
[...]
FICO is a leading analytics and software company renowned for its FICO® Score, a tool widely used by lenders to assess credit risk. The company offers a comprehensive platform that leverages data, AI, and machine learning to power intelligent decision-making and customer engagement across various industries. FICO's solutions span fraud detection, credit scoring, and customer lifecycle management, making it vital to sectors such as finance and telecommunications. Its innovative products help businesses optimize outcomes through real-time analytics, business composability, and scenario management.
[...]
• Design and deploy scalable backend systems and data pipelines that seamlessly integrate applied AI capabilities into FICO's analytics and decision management platform. • Build high-quality solutions for data ingestion, transformation, and storage, ensuring reliable, high-throughput data flow for real-time analytics. • Design and implement LLM-powered solutions for decision automation, fraud investigation, and process automation within FICO's platform. • Develop sophisticated prompting strategies and Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) architectures tailored to high-stakes, mission-critical applications. • Partner with data scientists, ML engineers, and product teams to develop microservices and APIs that enable intelligent, data-driven decision-making. • Optimize distributed architectures and implement real-time processing frameworks to support high-volume, low-latency workloads. • Implement advanced monitoring, testing, and performance optimization techniques to ensure system reliability, security, and scalability. • Define and evolve architectural patterns that support FICO's analytics and decisioning solutions at scale. • Lead and mentor engineering team members, promoting best practices in software development, data engineering, AI integration, and systems design.
[...]
• 7+ years of experience in backend or data engineering, with a demonstrated track record of delivering complex, large-scale production systems. • Strong coding skills in Python, Go, Java, or equivalent languages, with a commitment to clean, maintainable, and well-tested code. • Hands-on experience with big data frameworks (e.g., Apache Spark, Kafka, Hadoop) and a strong understanding of both relational and NoSQL databases. • Experience with cloud platforms (AWS, GCP, or Azure) and container orchestration tools such as Docker and Kubernetes. • Proven ability to design, build, and maintain scalable, production-grade backend systems with a focus on reliability and performance. • Solid experience with testing frameworks (including A/B testing), performance optimization, and production monitoring/observability practices. • Demonstrated experience integrating AI/ML solutions into production systems, including working with Large Language Models (LLMs) and related techniques. • Experience with Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) architectures and familiarity with vector databases (e.g., Pinecone, Weaviate, pgvector) is strongly preferred. • Strong problem-solving and communication skills, with the ability to mentor engineers, influence technical direction, and collaborate effectively across disciplines. • Bachelor's or Master's degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or a related field. An advanced degree is a plus but not required.
